rulings.Case Title: JOE DRUMMOND et al VS. FRANCESCA ASSOCIATION, A CALIFORNIA MUTUAL BENEFIT et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Further Responses To Reitman'S Request For Production Of Documents
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, November 6, 2014, Line 1, PETITIONER JOE DRUMMOND, JOYCE REITMAN Motion To Compel Further Responses To Reitman'S Request For Production Of Documents. Pro Tem Judge Conor Moore, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing Required. Parties should be prepared to discuss: (1) Is the sole issue in dispute whether the Association must produce an unredacted general ledger?; (2) Is this issue properly resolved as a discovery dispute or based on a finding on the merits of the parties claims? Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sfjudgeprotem@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: ROY MILLER et al VS. MARCUS DANIEL MERCHASIN et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Motion By Deft Marcus Daniel Merchasin In Support Of Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Document Production Demands By Pltff Mario Galande And Request For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $2475 Against Pltff Mario Galande And His Attorney
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, November 6, 2014, Line 2, DEFENDANT MARCUS MERCHASIN Motion By Defendant Marcus Daniel Merchasin In Support Of Motion To Compel Further Responses To Document Production Demands By Plaintiff Mario Galande And Request For Monetary Sanctions In The Amount Of $2475 Against Plaintiff Mario Galande And His Attorney. Pro Tem Judge Conor Moore, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's motion to compel further responses to requests for production of documents is granted. Plaintiff shall provide code-compliant, verified further responses to all requests for production in which Plaintiff failed to state that the responsive documents included those under Plaintiff's custody or control, and not simply in Plaintiff's possession. If there are additional documents in Plaintiff's custody or control that have not yet been produced, those documents shall be produced when the further responses are served. Plaintiff shall provide code-compliant, verified further responses to requests for production Nos. 16, 27, 28, and 37. Plaintiff's responses to these requests are not intelligible and are non-responsive. Plaintiff shall provide a privilege log for all documents withheld from production related to request for production No. 52. Plaintiff's further responses and any additional documents required by this order shall be served on Defendant no later than November 17, 2014. Plaintiff opposed Defendant's motion without justification and does not appear to have met his obligations to meet and confer on these disputes. Accordingly, Plaintiff and Plaintiff's counsel are sanctioned, jointly and severally, in the amount of $1,000, payable to Defendant no later than December 8, 2014. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sfjudgeprotem@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: REGENCY CAPITAL PARTNERS, LLC VS. ELAJOU GROUP, LLC et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication As To The First Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Thursday, November 6, 2014, Line 5, DEFENDANTs ELAJOU GROUP, LLC and JUMA ELAJOU's Motion For Summary Judgment, Or In The Alternative, For Summary Adjudication As To The First Amended Complaint. Denied. The Declaration of Mark D. Hill submitted in support of the opposition to the motion raises a triable issue of material fact as to whether Regency participated in the negotiations with Starwood Capital Group or whether it was solely acting as a finder and therefore within the finder's exception. (Hill Decl., pars. 9-14.) Defendants' request to strike Plaintiff's opposition is denied. Defendants cannot establish that they were prejudiced by the late-filed opposition, as the Court continued the original hearing date to provide Defendants with sufficient time to file a substantive reply brief. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: YOSHABEL CLEMENTS VS. HARCOURT GROUP, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Cross Defendant Richard Revel'S Demurrer To Cross-Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Thursday, November 6, 2014, Line 6, Cross Defendant Richard Revel'S Demurrer To Cross-Complaint. The demurrer is overruled. Mr. Revel did not file a "demurrer" and the notice of demurrer refers to CCP 410.10 [jurisdiction] as the basis for the filing and that provision is inapposite. In addition, even if the court takes judicial notice of the deed and concludes that the 10/9/11 transfer date is not reasonably subject to dispute, the cross-complaints still state a claim. Reading the cross-complaints liberally per CCP 452, they both allege a basis for liability other than Mr. Revel's legal ownership of the property. Paragraphs 17 of both cross-complaints allege that he was equitable owner and also had a role in the management/operation of the property. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: JULIE WALKER VS. PACIFIC GAS & ELECTRIC COMPANY, et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, General, Set Two, And Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, November 6, 2014, Line 7, DEFENDANT DANNY BUIST, PACIFIC GAS AND ELECTRIC COMPANY Motion To Compel Further Responses To Form Interrogatories, General, Set Two, And Request For Monetary Sanctions. Pro Tem Judge Conor Moore, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's motion to compel further responses to Form Interrogatory No. 17.1 with respect to Request for Admission (RFA) No. 43 is granted; Defendant's motion to compel further responses to Form Interrogatory No. 17.1 with respect to RFA Nos. 49 and 72 is denied. Plaintiff's argument that the form interrogatories must be served contemporaneously with the requests for admissions is without legal authority and would defeat the purposes of discovery. Plaintiff did make a substantive response to Request for Admission No. 43, which requires that she answer the form interrogatory. On the other hand, the form interrogatories do not make sense in light of the objections Plaintiff interposed to RFA Nos. 49 and 72. In light of the ruling, Plaintiff had substantial justification in opposing the motion. Accordingly, Defendant's motion for sanctions is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sfjudgeprotem@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: FOODCARE INC., A DELAWARE CORPORATION (FORMERLY VS. FOODCALC LLC, A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Ntc Of Mtn And Mtn To Compel Further Responses To Inspection Demands And For Sanctions Against Pltf And Its Attorney Of Record, Ali A Aalaei In The Amount Of $7,360.00
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, November 6, 2014, Line 4, DEFENDANT NEEDHAM, LUCY (SUED AS LUCY NEEDHAM A.K.A. LUCY Motion To Compel Further Responses To Inspection Demands And For Sanctions Against Plaintiff And Its Attorney Of Record, Ali A Aalaei In The Amount Of $7,360.00. Pro Tem Judge Conor Moore, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Defendant's motion to compel further responses to her inspection demands is granted. Plaintiff provided a series of generalized and boilerplate objections to the requests for production and then explicitly noted that documents were only being produced "subject to" these objections. Apparently, Plaintiff does not stand on these objections, further emphasizing the frivolous nature of these boilerplate objections. Defendant is entitled to know, in a verified response, that all responsive documents have been produced and, if not, which documents are being withheld and on what basis those documents are being withheld. Plaintiff's further verified responses to Defendant's requests for production shall be served on Defendant no later than November 17, 2014. Nevertheless, Defendant does not appear to have adequately met and conferred with Plaintiff on this matter before seeking court intervention. This failure did not allow Plaintiff to address the deficiencies in its responses, which would have prevented the necessity of the motion and the parties' expense. Defendant gave Plaintiff absolutely no indication until the day of filing that it was seeking a motion to compel, which was inappropriate in light of Defendant's production of documents and willingness to mediate the discovery dispute. Accordingly, Defendant and Defendant's counsel are sanctioned, jointly and severally, in the amount of $1,000, payable to Plaintiff no later than December 8, 2014. See Cal. Code of Civ. Proc. §§ 2023.010(i); 2023.020. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sfjudgeprotem@gmail.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: NINA PINZARRONE VS. STATE OF CALIFORNIA - A SOVEREIGN STATE et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Thursday, November 6, 2014, Line 11, DEFENDANT UNITED PARCEL SERVICE, INC.'s MOTION TO STRIKE 1ST AMENDED COMPLAINT. Granted without leave to amend. The following portions of the First Amended Complaint ("FAC") are hereby stricken: (1) That portion of Paragraph 116, Lines 12 to 13 which reads "Therefore, Pinzarrone seeks an award of punitive damages;" (2) Paragraphs 108 through 116, inclusive; and (3) Paragraph D of the Prayer on page 18 as to Defendant United Parcel Service, Inc. The request for punitive damages is stricken because the FAC does not plead facts establishing that Defendant acted with malice, oppression, or fraud. (See Civ. Code sec. 3294.) The allegations in paragraphs 108 through 116 are stricken as irrelevant. The motion is granted without leave to amend in light of the fact that Plaintiff was previously granted leave to amend to state facts to support a claim for punitive damages. Although the motion was not filed within the time permitted by CCP sec. 435, CCP sec. 436 provides that the Court may strike a pleading "at any time at its discretion." Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EHG)

rulings.Case Title: CARL PAYNE VS. JOHN AZEVEDO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Thursday, November 6, 2014, Line 12, DEFENDANT GREG VAN DYKE's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. The Demurrer is overruled. Plaintiff has sufficiently alleged a cause of action for willful misconduct. The Motion to Strike is denied. Plaintiff has alleged facts supporting the allegation of malicious conduct sufficiently to support a claim for punitive damages. rulings.Case Title: MARIO F OROPEZA VS. MICHAEL WILLIAM MORTENSEN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar on Thursday, November 6, 2014, Line 15, DEFENDANTs MICHAEL MORTENSEN and MARIA MORTENSEN's MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT. The motion is sustained with 10 days leave to amend. The Complaint fails to plead facts sufficient to support a claim for exemplary damages because the pleaded facts do not demonstrate the Defendants acted with oppression, fraud, or malice pursuant to Civil Code section 3294(a).
